title: Smoky French Buckwheat Noodles
description: A cultured and chewy French dish featuring strong wholemeal flour, this macrobiotic-friendly recipe is a great hangover cure. It is cooked to perfection in a bamboo steamer.
instructions: Bring the vegetable broth to a boil in a large pot.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and add the smoked paprika, garlic, onion, and mushrooms.
In a large bowl, whisk together the buckwheat flour and a pinch of salt.
Gradually add the hot broth to the flour mixture, stirring with a fork until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a long rope.
Cut the rope into 1/4 inch thick slices to form the noodles.
Place the noodles in a bamboo steamer lined with parchment paper.
Steam the noodles over boiling water for 10 minutes.
Drizzle the noodles with olive oil and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
